license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6nvhde6
title: Freezing and processing intestinal biopsies for the isolation of CD45+ leukocytes
author: Oxford Hca

steps_raw.instruction: Collect endoscopic biopsies into 50ml falcon tube with 5ml of cold MACS tissue storage solution.
Ensure all biopsies are immersed in the solution.
Keep on ice until ready to freeze.
Move onto Stage 2 as soon as possible, within 6 hours maximum.
Perform all stages in the hood.
Ensure that a CoolCell LX cell freezing container is available, and is defrosted to room temperature.
Label a cryovial for each sample, as appropriate.
Add 1000μl of Cs10 to cryovial.
Carefully remove all MACS tissue storage solution from the 50ml falcon with a pastette (transfer pipette) and P1000 pipette, taking care not to disrupt or discard the biopsies.
Using sterile forceps, transfer the biopsies into the Cs10-containing cryovial.
Place cryovials into CoolCell LX and transfer to -80 C freezer.
Move to Stage 3 on the next working day if possible.
Remove sample from CoolCell LX and transfer to liquid nitrogen box (if for long-term storage), or to -80 C box (if planned for use in next 2 months).
Perform all stages in the hood.
Warm Complete Media and Digestion Media to 37 C prior to beginning.
Proceed quickly to remove the biopsies from the freezing media.
Fill a gentleMACS C tube with 5 ml of Digestion Media.
Fill an appropriately labelled 15 ml conical with 9 ml of Complete Media.
Begin to thaw the frozen biopsy in a 37 C waterbath.
When the frozen biopsy begins to thaw, but haven’t thawed completely, remove from the water bath and transfer to the hood.
Finish thawing the biopsy by adding 1 ml of Complete Media (from the 15 ml conical) dropwise.
Use a pastette to transfer the media and biopsies into the appropriate 15 ml conical.
Place a 70 micron cell strainer onto a 50 ml conical.
Pour the biopsies onto the strainer.
Wash with 20 ml of Complete Media.
Use sterile forceps to transfer the biopsies into a gentleMACS C tube filled with 5 ml of Digestion Media.
Homogenize on the gentleMACS using programme brain 01_02 (gentle). Tissue tends to get caught in the rotor blade of the gentleMACS tube, use pastette to return to bottom of tube.
Place in a 37 C shaking incubator (220 rpm) for 1 h.
Homogenize on the gentleMACS using programme m_intestine_01.
Strain cells through 70µm filter into 50mL falcon tube. Pour onto filter.

Grind remaining tissue (use plunger from a syringe to break up any clumps on top of filter (pestle and mortar-action). Wash any cells around blades, on filter or still in tube using Complete Media and a pastette.
Centrifuge at 600 g for 10 minutes at 4 C.
Resuspend in 20 ml of Complete Media.
Centrifuge at 600 g for 5 minutes at 4 C.
Resuspend cell pellet in 6mL of 35% Percoll.
Transfer to 15mL falcon tube.
Underlay 3 mL of 70% Percoll solution using a glass Pasteur pipette.
Centrifuge at 800xg for 20 minutes (at RT) without the brake (decrease acceleration to 7).

Take the layer between 35% and 70% Percoll and transfer to a new 15mL falcon tube. Before removing interphase, remove some of the top layer to avoid stromal cell contamination of mononuclear fraction.
Fill the tube to 15 ml with Complete Media.
Centrifuge at 600xg for 5 minutes.
Remove supernatant.
Resuspend cell pellet in 10mL Complete Media.
Count cells using trypan blue.
Cells are now ready for downstream applications
